title: Georgia AG — Travis Johnson / LinkedIn Post Coordination

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any and all communications between Travis Johnson, Chief of Staff for the Office of the Attorney General, and Attorney General Chris Carr — or between Travis Johnson and any other employee of the Office of the Attorney General — regarding either of the following:

1. The LinkedIn post published from the Attorney General's official LinkedIn account on or about April 11, 2026; or

2. Any request by Flock Safety, Bob Carter, or any Flock Safety representative for a like, repost, comment, or other social media engagement from the Attorney General or the Office of the Attorney General.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by email attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

If you contend that any responsive records are exempt from disclosure, please provide a written explanation identifying the specific statutory basis for withholding each record individually.

Please provide records in their original electronic format, including all available metadata.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jason Hunyar

title: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Georgia AG — Travis Johnson / Flock Safety Op-Ed Draft

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any op-ed, draft op-ed, talking points, memoranda, or related communications prepared by Travis Johnson, Chief of Staff for the Office of the Attorney General, or any other employee of the Office of the Attorney General, in response to or in connection with public criticism of Flock Safety or its technology, from January 1, 2025 through the present.

This request is based on a text message sent by Mr. Johnson on April 11, 2026, in which he stated that the Office of the Attorney General was "drafting an op-ed" in connection with a LinkedIn post published by Bob Carter, Vice President of Flock Safety.

This request includes but is not limited to: any drafts in any stage of completion, any communications between staff members regarding the drafting or approval of such content, and any final versions whether or not they were ultimately published.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by email attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

If you contend that any responsive records are exempt from disclosure, please provide a written explanation identifying the specific statutory basis for withholding each record individually.

Please provide records in their original electronic format, including all available metadata

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jason Hunyar

title: Georgia AG — Travis Johnson / Flock Safety & Impact Public Affairs Communications

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All communications — including text messages, emails, direct messages, iMessages, Signal messages, WhatsApp messages, or any other written or electronic communications — between Travis Johnson, Chief of Staff for the Office of the Attorney General, and any individual sending or receiving communications from a @flocksafety.com or @impactpublicaffairs.com email address, on any government-issued device or government account, from January 1, 2026 through the present.

Please exclude marketing emails and automated system notifications from Flock Safety from the production.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

Sincerely,

Jason Hunyar

title: Joe Seconder Calls Before Flock Meeting

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Joe Seconder Calls Before Flock Meeting
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I hereby request the following records for Dunwoody City Councilman Joe Seconder for April 11 and April 12, 2026:

All call logs and phone records for any city-issued phone assigned to Councilman Seconder
All voicemails received or sent on any city-issued phone assigned to Councilman Seconder

If any portion of this request cannot be fulfilled in its entirety, please provide all records that are immediately available and identify with specificity any records being withheld and the statutory exemption relied upon for each, as required by O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(d). Please do not withhold the entire production because one category of records may be subject to an exemption.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jason Hunyar
